opp.name: ACEN S2P
opp.next_step: 50% Probability 4th May- reached out to Zee fr lunch and meeting with Globe about Agentic AI 16th Mar - Sent an intro mail and requesting for a revival meeting. 14th Jan Push Zee now for submission of our proposal in next exco meeting end Jan 30th Oct Zee has advised management (exco) have decided to delay for 1 quarter 27th Oct Revised proposal- to meet Zee week of 12th Nov 14th Oct Zee indicates if we can work within a budget that he can get through exco, we may be able to close off in Q3- Carl to revise numbers and work with Zee 1:1 to optimize (likely reduce scope) 22nd Sept: President Zee has confirmed that due to ongoing challenges with other initiatives, the Procurement Digitization project will be postponed by a quarter minimum . Budget creation and resource allocation are currently not feasible but he will look into it at earliest possible. They plan to reconnect in January 2026 to revisit the initiative. In the meantime, we will maintain a warm engagement , given the significant groundwork already completed, so we can seamlessly resume when the timing is right. The prospect is still interested in Zycus. 11th Sept: Carl has agreed to send revised commercials to Zee which we will prepare and send by next week. 8th Sept: Acen requested for revised proposal which includes recently discussed integration touch points which we will send it by EOD. 1st Sep Carl meeting Zee (CPO) 5th Sept to push for close- and remove blockers for Q2 close 26th Aug: Our team is preparing for 4th SAept meeting, no other update from Acen side. 18th Aug: Architecture Walkthrough meeting is scheduled on 4th Sept. 13th Aug: Jeff arranged quick clarification meeting for us to set the expectation for a meeting to discuss " Detailed walkthrough of the full system architecture, starting from supplier processes, through the Source-to-Pay (S2P) application layer, and down to the SAP ERP system ." 12th Aug: Since Jenn is on leave till 14th Aug, meeting is postponed to new date. Jeff will confirm new schedule soon. 8th Aug: Meeting scheduled for 13th Aug. 31st July: Requested for a clarification meeting with our presales. Awaiting for Acen's availability. 28th July: Acen has requested for a detailed walkthrough of the full system architecture, starting from supplier processes, through the Source-to-Pay (S2P) application layer, and down to the SAP ERP system. 16th July: In the meeting with Jenn, she concluded that she will get back to us after reviewing SAP scenarios and cost associated with it. 14th July: Jenn has set up a meeting to discuss SAP secondary cost on 15th July. Jeff is still chasing for ref call with SM. 30th June: Project has been delayed to Q1,2026.Onsite meeting with Acen team and President is scheduled for 9th July. 23rd June: Jeff and Jenn are negotiating with SAP for the secondary cost. meeting them in Manila on 25th June. 18th June: Acen is working on the user count and requested a session on 23rd to help them preparing exco presentations. We need to work on competitive analysis for Ariba. 9th June: We have a meeting today with CIP,CPO today to discuss revisions in the proposal and clarifications they have requested. 3rd June: Acen has confirmed that Zycus is shortlisted as top 2. (Other one is SAP-Ariba). Acen has requested for a isaas advance clarification session to plan internal resource planning and secondary cost from SAP Middleware. 2nd June: Acen responded with comments on our proposal and few additional queries related to commercials. Response expected by 6th June. Invoice page limit can be a blocker. 29th May The SAP blocker has caused some internal issues with final award timing- Carl to try and find a way to work around this slow down 26th May Arranging call for Carl and Zee to talk through how Zycus can better assist in TCO (if in fact SAP are playing this low integrity card of charging a fee to integrate Zycus) 22nd May: Had a call with President - Zee who shared a concerned over SAP secondary integration cost which is significant. He is yet to see the evaluation summary and commercials from Mehr. 19th May: Mehr mentioned that they havent finished compiling responses from all the vendors. 12th May: Jenn (CIO) and Mehr (CPO) are discussing the revisions they need in user count. We have another round of clarification on 14th May. 7th May: We had a first round proposal clarification. Acen is discussing internally the revisions they need in the proposal from our end.Expecting next clarification meeting in the week of 12th May. 5th May: Having a meeting with Zee this week, he is yet to confirm the time. RFP Committee has acknowledged the proposal and requested for a week to review it before any meeting or discussion. 28th April- Final proposal2 sending today- commercials and contract discussions planned for week of 5th May 5th April: Jeff extended the RFP response submission timeline to 30th April. Proposal is ready to be submitted from our side. 21st April: Progressing on RFP response with help of Carl,AKshay and Tanvi. To be submitted on 24th April. 14th April: Working on proposal preparation which is due on 24th April. POC helped us in getting better clarity of their requirements and expectations. 11th April: Successfully delivered the onsite POC in presence of Jenn (CIO) and Mehr(CPO). 8th April: Secured a face to face meeting for Carl and I to meet CPO Mehr and President along with CIO on 11th April. 7th April: Acen CIO Jenn had responded with selection criteria table as a response to my request. Weightage is 50% for functional fit and 50% for tech capabilities and ROI. Working with Carl on winning strategy. 4th April: Requested Shravan for a data migration partner which we need for this project. Working with presales on POC demo 31st Mar - Closed plan attached. 28th March - We have submitted the clarifications questions for RFP and POC preparation. 14th Mar- Acen has invited us for participating in S2P RFP. More details will be added in the notes. 10th Mar- Zee, Jonathan,Carl and Madhuri dinner meeting is set for 17th Mar 6th Mar- Zee requested our RFP template which he can ask his team to use without telling its coming from Zycus. 27th Feb: Carl, Zee and Madhuri spoke regarding fastening the vendor selection process, Zee, his boss (CFO - Jonnathan Back) are backing Zycus. Zee will speak to team to get the vendor selection done by April end. 21st Feb: Demo was interactive, CIO and CPO were clarifying functional and process related queries.Interest in ESG module as well. Lythouse deck and demo request has been sent. 18th Feb: we have scheduled a demo on 21st Feb for Acen CPO,CIO and key stakeholders to understand Zycus product better before they initiate RFI. 13th Feb strong F2F meeting with CPO (is Zycus champion) will move forward with project now- met with extended management team, finance, procurement, IT- strong position here following sister company close (CGash) 3rd Feb: Acen CPO Jenn is taking help in coaching her team regarding IMPL with HANA, phasewise impl approach, tender template etc. 24th Jan: Both Acen President and CPO have tentatively accepted Horizon invitation. To be confirmed on 5th Feb meeting with Carl and Madhuri. 21st Jan: As per Jenn, there is a slight delay in RFP roll out. She is pushing it for mid feb. 20th Jan: CPO meeting has been reschduled to 5th Feb since CPO also wanted to include other BU stakeholders. 13th Jan: Acen President and CPO has sent invite to Carl and I for a meeting on 22nd Jan for discussing GCASH Success and next steps. 16th Dec: Madhuri and Jeff had a call and he confirmed to release RFP in Jan'25. 4th Dec: Madhuri met Acen President, CPO, IT Head at their office. They are favouring Zycus as they have heard great things about Zycus from Delta air friends and from ABoitiz power. As part of the process, they will issue an RFP in Feb,25 6th Nov: IT and Procurement team from Acen is actively engaging with us. Detailed demo was done today and follow up clarification session is scheduled for 11th Nov. 25th OCt: To sign the NDA and get Pain-points, requirements and process information from them.
opp.ais_why:
• Per the 4 May 2026 rep note: 'reached out to Zee fr lunch and meeting with Globe about Agentic AI.' This is a very recent, brief signal that the rep is bridging ACEN with Globe Telecom for an Agentic AI conversation ‚Äî Zee (champion) is the ACEN-side touch and Globe Telecom is the AI-anchor reference.
• Per the 14 Jan rep note: 'Push Zee now for submission of our proposal in next exco meeting end Jan.' Per the 30 Oct 2024 note: 'Zee has advised management (exco) have decided to delay for 1 quarter.' ACEN is a long-running multi-stage SAP-integration-heavy S2P deal with Zee as champion, vendor-selected stage reached but exco delays and SAP architecture walkthroughs dominate. Per 22 Sep 2024 from President Zee: 'due to ongoing challenges with other initiatives, the Procurement Digitization project will be postponed by a quarter minimum.'
• Confidence is Medium given the extremely rich NextStep (8,099 chars over ~20 months). Product scope is iContract; iManage; iSupplier; eProcurement; eInvoicing; iRisk; Lythouse ‚Äî no AI SKUs. The single AI keyword is the May 2026 reference to Globe Telecom's Agentic AI conversation as a future bridge. Score 4 (AI Curious, boundary DOWN) reflects the lack of AI products in scope, the SAP-integration focus, and the recent Agentic AI side-conversation that has not yet matured.

items.quote: Zee shared a concern over SAP secondary integration cost which is significant.

sources: Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (Zee, ~70% non-PO spend)
Avoma call 6 May 2025 (user-count and invoice-volume commercials)
SF Next_Step__c 7 Apr 2025 (50% functional / 50% tech+ROI weighting)
metrics.narrative: Pain is well articulated but never quantified. The 28 Feb 2025 call with the relevant stakeholder surfaced ~70% of ACEN's spend as non-PO (loan and tax payments) creating a 'manual disconnect' from SAP, and the 6 May 2025 proposal-clarification call detailed user counts (35 buyers, ~170 approvers, 300 requesters, 30,000 invoices over a 5-year term) but no cost, headcount or cycle-time savings figure. With ACEN's own selection weighting at 50% functional fit and 50% technical capability plus ROI (SF Next_Step__c 7 Apr 2025), a quantified value case is the missing lever the exco needs to justify spend against the SAP secondary-integration cost, and the wedge that neutralizes Ariba's TCO edge. Quantifying ROI is a ranked move.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 6 Mar 2025 (insider RFP-template request), 27 Feb 2025 ('backing Zycus'), 30 Oct 2025 (last Zee signal)
SF activity 24 Mar 2026 (Zee declines event), 4 May and 15 Jun 2026 (rep outreach unanswered)
Avoma call 13 Aug 2025 (Jeff driving operationally)
champion.narrative: Zee Pervez (President ACEN Shared Services) was a strong, active champion through Feb-Oct 2025: SF Next_Step__c 6 Mar 2025 'Zee requested our RFP template which he can ask his team to use without telling it's coming from Zycus' (clear insider advocacy) and 27 Feb 2025 'Zee, his boss (CFO Jonathan Back) are backing Zycus.' On the 28 Feb 2025 call he personally committed to pushing the exco and issuing the RFP. That advocacy has cooled: his last specific signal was the 30 Oct 2025 exco-delay note (~8 months ago), a 4 May 2026 lunch/Agentic-AI outreach and a 15 Jun 2026 Horizon invite are unanswered, and his only recent touch was declining an event on 24 Mar 2026. Jeff De Sagun (Senior IT Application Specialist, SF primary contact) is an engaged day-to-day operational driver but not an executive advocate. What developed: multi-thread depth across IT, procurement and finance and a top-2 position. What is missing: a re-activated executive champion pushing the steering/exco decision. This silence is the single biggest threat to the close.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 3 Jun 2025 (top-2 vs SAP Ariba)
SF Competitors__c (Coupa; GEP Worldwide; iValua; SAP Ariba)
SF Next_Step__c 22-29 May 2025 (SAP secondary cost)
Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (in-house P2P incumbent, D&B verification)
competition.narrative: Two-horse race down-selected to Zycus and SAP Ariba on 3 Jun 2025 (SF Next_Step__c: 'Zycus is shortlisted as top 2. Other one is SAP-Ariba'). Ariba's structural edge is being SAP-native, which surfaces as ACEN's 'significant' SAP secondary integration/middleware cost concern (Zee, 22 May 2025) - a genuine TCO disadvantage for Zycus on a SAP ERP shop. Zycus's wedge is functional and AI depth: Merlin AI contract risk-scoring and invoice OCR, non-SAP-locked configurable roles, a free unlimited supplier portal (6 May 2025), the non-PO payment-module coverage Ariba was not asked about, and references (BDO, SM offered 13 Aug 2025). Coupa, GEP Worldwide and iValua sit on Competitors__c but never recur in any call - earlier-round names now faded. The larger current threat than any vendor is do-nothing / repeated deferral driven by budget contention with ACEN's HR-ERP and billing programs.

sources: Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (~70% non-PO disconnect, in-house P2P, ADB/ESG)
Avoma call 13 Aug 2025 (supplier-to-SAP architecture pain)
SF Products (replacing in-house P2P)
identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed, specific operational pain owned by procurement and finance. ACEN is replacing an in-house-built P2P system whose ~70% of spend (non-PO loan and tax payments) is disconnected from SAP and handled manually (Zee, 28 Feb 2025: 'there's a manual disconnect'). Zee framed the strategic driver as institutionalizing 'from a Filipino energy company to a global energy company,' with concurrent HR-ERP and gen-billing projects and ADB funding imposing supplier code-of-conduct/ESG requirements. The named pain owners are procurement (Head of Procurement Mher Tolentino-Blas, CPO context) and IT (Jennelyn Nicolas, Jeff De Sagun, Aaron Angeles). The pain is real and endorsed at President level; what is missing is a compelling event, since the project was explicitly not in the 2025 budget and keeps being deferred behind other initiatives.

sources: Avoma call 13 Aug 2025 (Order Form/MSA to Jeff for legal pre-review)
SF Next_Step__c 22-29 May 2025 (SAP secondary-integration cost blocker)
Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (5-year term, first-year amortization, security review)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partially in motion but stalled. On the 13 Aug 2025 call Madhuri proposed sending the Order Form and MSA to Jeff De Sagun for ACEN legal pre-review, warning 'the legal really need a lot of time... it saves 3 or 4 weeks of legal back and forth later on,' and Jeff agreed to pass it to legal. No evidence the paper moved since. The forcing blockers are commercial not legal: the 'significant' SAP secondary-integration/middleware cost (Zee, 22 May 2025; SF 23-29 May 2025) and an invoice page-limit concern flagged 2 Jun 2025. Terms are a 5-year subscription with flexible first-year amortization offered by Carl on 28 Feb 2025. Visibility would improve by getting the Order Form/MSA formally into ACEN legal and a written InfoSec review scheduled (security named as a decision driver by Zee, 28 Feb 2025).

sources: Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (Zee names CFO Jonathan Back, 4-person decision group)
SF OpportunityContactRole (Jonathan Back = Executive Sponsor)
SF Next_Step__c 27 Feb 2025 ('CFO Jonathan Back backing Zycus')
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identified but never directly engaged on a recorded call. Jonathan Back (CFO and Chief Strategy Officer, SF Executive Sponsor role) holds final budget: on the 28 Feb 2025 call Zee said 'my boss, CFO of the corporation is a Brit, Oxford grad, from JPMorgan' and that 'my CFO is going to be very laser sharp' on the non-PO payment requirement, and SF Next_Step__c 27 Feb 2025 records 'Zee, his boss (CFO Jonathan Back) are backing Zycus.' Zee (President ACEN Shared Services) controls the commercial conversation day to day and told us 'I'm part of that management team... 4 people who are gonna take the decision' (28 Feb 2025). The gap is that Back has never appeared on a call and the budget was explicitly not planned for 2025 (Zee, 28 Feb 2025), so re-confirming his sponsorship and the funded budget path is the open EB action.

sources: Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (Zee: 4-person decision group, RFP timeline)
Avoma call 13 Aug 2025 (steering-committee gate sequence)
SF Next_Step__c 3 Jun 2025 (top-2 down-select) and 30 Oct 2025 (exco delay)
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal RFP-to-steering-committee process that Zee drove top-down. Sequence from the calls and next steps: RFP invited 14 Mar 2025, POC delivered onsite 11 Apr 2025 for Jenn and CPO Mher, proposal submitted end-Apr 2025, commercial clarification 6 May 2025, down-select to top-2 vs SAP Ariba 3 Jun 2025, then an architecture-mapping and reference-and-legal sequence set 13 Aug 2025 before commercials and IT-steering-committee sign-off. Zee described the authority chain on 28 Feb 2025: a 4-person management group makes the call, his boss CFO Jonathan Back aligned, and he needed an RFP issued in March, results and a decision by mid-April. The process then stalled at the steering stage: exco deferred a quarter (30 Oct 2025) and no gate has closed since, so the process is intact but frozen pending re-prioritization.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 7 Apr 2025 (50/50 criteria table)
Avoma call 13 Aug 2025 (architecture-fit scoring)
Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (security, non-PO payment module, ESG/ADB)
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicitly documented by the buyer. On 7 Apr 2025 CIO-level lead Jennelyn Nicolas sent a selection-criteria table weighting the evaluation 50% functional fit and 50% technical capability plus ROI (SF Next_Step__c). The transcripts show the functional criteria in detail: end-to-end supplier-to-SAP process/architecture fidelity (13 Aug 2025 architecture-walkthrough scoping), configurable non-SAP-locked user roles and invoice-assignment rules (6 May 2025), security and data protection for the gen-AI components (28 Feb 2025, Zee: 'security... is going to be very heightened in our decision making'), and a specific non-PO 'payment module' / request-for-payment capability that covers ~70% of spend (28 Feb 2025). ESG/Lythouse is a secondary, ADB-driven requirement. The competitive criterion is TCO including the SAP secondary-integration cost.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. Strong on paper for the stage, but it's stalling — the 'what could lose it' block is the real story.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Vendor Selected' anchors near 72. Thin signals pull the read to 64.
blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Zee Pervez was a strong, active executive champion through Feb-Oct 2025 (insider RFP-template request 6 Mar 2025, 'backing Zycus' 27 Feb 2025) but has been silent ~8 months and unresponsive to 2026 outreach; Jeff De Sagun is an engaged operational driver, not an exec advocate. Champion is at risk and is the deal's root stall.
bullets.text: Champion is driving it — Zee Pervez was a strong, active executive champion through Feb-Oct 2025 (insider RFP-template request 6 Mar 2025, 'backing Zycus' 27 Feb 2025) but…

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Live but frozen two-horse race: Zycus vs SAP Ariba, down-selected 3 Jun 2025.

bullets.text: Repeated deferral behind concurrent HR-ERP and billing programs; project explicitly not in the 2025 budget, no compelling event to force a decision.
Champion Zee silent ~8 months and unresponsive; economic buyer Jonathan Back never directly engaged.
'Significant' SAP secondary-integration/middleware cost hands SAP Ariba a TCO edge and is unresolved.
Procurement transformation was not in ACEN's 2025 budget; exco funding path unconfirmed.

items: Carl re-engages Zee directly with a dated revival meeting tied to the Agentic-AI and Globe reference hook. (by 10 Jul)
Ask Jeff De Sagun for the current status of the architecture-mapping, reference and legal gates. (by 17 Jul)
Build a quantified TCO/ROI model that answers the SAP secondary-integration cost head-on. (by 1 Aug)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 64 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 60.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 39 is anchored on the stage win (64), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 4 / Negative (AI Curious): scope is SAP-integration heavy with no AI SKUs. Calls corroborate this - AI surfaces only as embedded Merlin features (contract risk, supplier risk, invoice OCR) that the buyer treated as line items, not as a driver. A May 2026 Agentic-AI side-conversation bridging ACEN with Globe Telecom has not matured.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only: ~70% of spend non-PO and disconnected from SAP, manual supplier onboarding, in-house P2P replacement (28 Feb 2025). No cost, headcount or cycle-time savings quantified by the buyer.

items.change: Down-selected to top-2, Zycus vs SAP Ariba
Architecture-walkthrough and reference/legal pre-review gates scoped, targeted end of month
Exco decided to delay the procurement-digitization project by one quarter
Last buyer-initiated touch: Zee declined an event invite by email; subsequent rep outreach unanswered

deal_movement.summary: The deal reached Vendor Selected (top-2 vs SAP Ariba, 3 Jun 2025) and progressed through a POC, proposal, commercial clarification and an architecture-walkthrough scoping (13 Aug 2025), then stalled at the steering/exco stage. Exco deferred a quarter (30 Oct 2025); the close date has been pushed repeatedly to 2027-01-31; ownership moved to Carl Kimball (28 Apr 2026). Buyer engagement has thinned to a single 24 Mar 2026 inbound email, with rep outreach on 4 May and 15 Jun 2026 unanswered. This is buyer deferral, not rep inactivity.

Champion is at risk and is the deal's root stall.

items.expected_effect: Restores operational momentum and reveals whether the stall is budget or process, informing the exco re-approach.

items: Last two-way buyer touch 24 Mar 2026 (inbound email declining an event).
Champion Zee silent ~8 months; two 2026 outreach attempts unanswered.
Three pre-award gates set 13 Aug 2025 still open ~10.5 months later.
confidence_signals.summary: Buyer engagement is cooling to cold: no substantive working call since 13 Aug 2025, no champion signal since 30 Oct 2025, and rep outreach on 4 May and 15 Jun 2026 unanswered after a 24 Mar 2026 polite decline.

north_star_verdict.math: Today 2026-07-03. Close 2027-01-31 = 212 days out. Stage Vendor Selected reached but time-in-stage exceeds ~13 months. Last two-way buyer touch was an inbound email 24 Mar 2026 (~101 days ago); last substantive working call 13 Aug 2025 (~10.5 months ago); last Salesforce activity 15 Jun 2026 is rep-side. Exco deferred at least once (30 Oct 2025). No path to the date without a fresh compelling event and re-activated executive advocacy.
evidence: SF Next_Step__c 3 Jun 2025: 'Acen has confirmed that Zycus is shortlisted as top 2. (Other one is SAP-Ariba)' - Vendor Selected, two-horse race.

Avoma 13 Aug 2025 (Jeff De Sagun): three pre-award gates sequenced (architecture mapping -> references -> legal), targeted 'before the end of this month' - no completion evidence in the ~10.5 months since.
SF Next_Step__c 30 Oct 2025: 'Zee has advised management (exco) have decided to delay for 1 quarter' - last Zee-specific signal, ~8 months ago.
SF activity 24 Mar 2026 (inbound email from Zee): polite decline of an event invite - the only buyer-initiated touch since Oct 2025; rep outreach 4 May and 15 Jun 2026 is unanswered.
Best Case with three open pre-award gates and an ~8-month-silent champion is not defensible; recommend Pipeline.
north_star_verdict.headline: Well-qualified top-2 deal (Zycus vs SAP Ariba) that has gone quiet on the buyer side. Three pre-award gates set on the 13 Aug 2025 call (architecture mapping, customer references, MSA legal pre-review) are still open ~10.5 months later, champion Zee has been silent since the 30 Oct 2025 exco-delay note, and the only buyer contact since is a 24 Mar 2026 email declining an event invite. Rep outreach on 4 May and 15 Jun 2026 is unanswered. This is buyer deferral, not rep inactivity, and re-engaging the buyer is the only real move.

flags: Re-activate the executive champion: Zee has been silent ~8 months and is the sole route to the exco decision; the owner must re-open the relationship with a specific value hook before any commercial step.
Answer the SAP secondary-integration TCO with a quantified model: it is Ariba's only structural edge and the exco's stated ROI criterion (50% weight); a numeric total-cost comparison neutralizes it.

Reach the economic buyer: CFO Jonathan Back holds budget, backed Zycus verbally in Feb 2025, but has never been on a call; securing his written sponsorship is what unfreezes a deferred, unbudgeted project.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a top-2, well-qualified deal that has gone cold on the buyer side; the win levers are re-activating executive advocacy, arming the exco with a quantified TCO/ROI answer to the SAP cost, and reaching the economic buyer.

customer_preference.evidence: Historically high: Zee praised Zycus differentiators and pricing flexibility ('that's exactly what distinguishing features mean... never heard before from a vendor', 28 Feb 2025) and requested our RFP template as an insider (6 Mar 2025). Decayed to medium - no recurrence in ~10.5 months of buyer silence.

competitive_position.summary: Live but frozen two-horse race: Zycus vs SAP Ariba, down-selected 3 Jun 2025. SAP Ariba is the single strongest current vendor threat because it is SAP-native and ACEN's 'significant' SAP secondary-integration cost (Zee, 22 May 2025) hands Ariba a TCO edge on a SAP ERP shop. The larger threat than any vendor is do-nothing / repeated exco deferral. The in-house-built P2P system is the incumbent being displaced. Coupa, GEP Worldwide and iValua sit on the Salesforce field but never recur in calls - faded earlier-round names.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Acen has confirmed that Zycus is shortlisted as top 2. (Other one is SAP-Ariba).
competitors.how_we_win: Neutralize the SAP secondary-integration-cost narrative with a quantified TCO/ROI model and lead with Merlin AI risk-scoring plus non-SAP-locked configuration.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / project deferral
competitors.quote: President Zee has confirmed that due to ongoing challenges with other initiatives, the Procurement Digitization project will be postponed by a quarter minimum.
competitors.how_we_win: Re-establish a compelling event and an exco-funded budget path; the bigger threat than any vendor is repeated deferral.

competitors.name: In-house developed P2P system (incumbent)
competitors.quote: 70% of my spend is non purchase order... there's a manual disconnect.
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the ~70% non-PO disconnect and manual supplier onboarding as the cost of standing still.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Not raised in any call; monitor only.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: Not raised in any call; monitor only.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: Not raised in any call; monitor only.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin AI story exists (AI contract risk-scoring, AI-based supplier risk assessment, invoice OCR) and was explained on the 6 May 2025 call, but it has never been carried to the exco/steering level as a differentiator, and no AI SKUs are in the product scope. The AI narrative is under-leveraged given a 50% technical/ROI criterion.

items.quote: Everything is part of it... think of this as that additional payment module.
items.criterion: Functional fit (S2P coverage, non-PO payment module, configurability)

items.quote: detailed walkthrough of the full system architecture, from supplier processes through the S2P layer down to the SAP ERP system.
Zee shared a concern over SAP secondary integration cost which is significant.
items.criterion: ROI / TCO including SAP secondary-integration cost

items.quote: security... is going to be very heightened in our decision making.
items.criterion: Security / data protection for gen-AI components

customer_expectations_fit.summary: ACEN weighted the evaluation 50% functional fit and 50% technical capability plus ROI (7 Apr 2025). Zycus is aligned on functional fit and process/architecture coverage but exposed on quantified ROI and on the SAP secondary-integration TCO, which is Ariba's leverage.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the 4 May 2026 rep note: 'reached out to Zee fr lunch and meeting with Globe about Agentic AI.' This is a very recent, brief signal that the rep is bridging ACEN with Globe Telecom for an Agentic AI conversation ‚Äî Zee (champion) is the ACEN-side touch and Globe Telecom is the AI-anchor reference.
• Per the 14 Jan rep note: 'Push Zee now for submission of our proposal in next exco meeting end Jan.' Per the 30 Oct 2024 note: 'Zee has advised management (exco) have decided to delay for 1 quarter.' ACEN is a long-running multi-stage SAP-integration-heavy S2P deal with Zee as champion, vendor-selected stage reached but exco delays and SAP architecture walkthroughs dominate. Per 22 Sep 2024 from President Zee: 'due to ongoing challenges with other initiatives, the Procurement Digitization project will be postponed by a quarter minimum.'
• Confidence is Medium given the extremely rich NextStep (8,099 chars over ~20 months). Product scope is iContract; iManage; iSupplier; eProcurement; eInvoicing; iRisk; Lythouse ‚Äî no AI SKUs. The single AI keyword is the May 2026 reference to Globe Telecom's Agentic AI conversation as a future bridge. Score 4 (AI Curious, boundary DOWN) reflects the lack of AI products in scope, the SAP-integration focus, and the recent Agentic AI side-conversation that has not yet matured.

22nd Sept: President Zee has confirmed that due to ongoing challenges with other initiatives, the Procurement Digitization project will be postponed by a quarter minimum. Budget creation and resource allocation are currently not feasible but he will look into it at earliest possible.

They plan to reconnect in January 2026 to revisit the initiative. In the meantime, we will maintain a warm engagement, given the significant groundwork already completed, so we can seamlessly resume when the timing is right. The prospect is still interested in Zycus.
